svg.text: LSF — LANGUE DES SIGNES FRANCAISE
CHEROLOGY — ENLIGHTENMENT-ERA SIGN SYSTEMS
LSF CHEROLOGY (PHONOLOGY)
LSF parameters: handshape, location, movement, orientation, NMM
Same five parameters as all sign languages (universal feature of SLs)
LSF handshapes: many are same as or related to ASL handshapes (shared ancestor)
LSF DISTINCTIVE FEATURES
Initialized signs: handshape = first letter of French word (common in LSF)
FAMILLE (family): F-handshape • MONDE (world): M-handshape
Initialization in LSF carried into ASL (some ASL signs also initialized)
LSF NMM
Mouth morphemes in LSF: French word mouthed simultaneously with signs
Eyebrows: same grammatical functions as all sign languages
Gaze: important in LSF spatial grammar (like all sign languages)
LSF REGIONAL VARIATION
Paris: de facto standard • regional variants throughout France
Belgian French Sign Language (LSFB): related but distinct from LSF
DGS CHEROLOGY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
DGS PHONOLOGICAL SYSTEM
DGS: same five-parameter system as LSF and all sign languages
DGS handshapes: some overlap with LSF/ASL, some unique to German tradition
DGS compounds: combine two signs to form new concept (very productive)
LSF VS DGS: SIGN COMPARISON
LEARN: LSF and DGS have different signs for this concept
HOUSE: different signs (iconic but differ in which features are selected)
Despite shared educational history: most content signs are distinct
SHARED CHEROLOGY
Both: same five parameters (universal to all sign languages)
Both: similar NMM grammar (eyebrows, gaze, head movement)
Both: one-handed manual alphabet (shared French tradition)
PLATE 64b ALT · LSF / DGS · CHEROLOGY
